Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows evidence of renal impairment (elevated creatinine), proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, elevated protein, glucose, bilirubin, and ketones). Hypoglycemia is present with low HbA1c, suggesting chronic low glucose. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Findings are consistent with possible nephrotic syndrome or advanced diabetic nephropathy, but diabetes control appears overly tight or malnutrition is present. Recommend urgent clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation.
